note: V13 NTP schema semantic cleanup. (A) schemas/trinity/v13_release_candidate_report.schema.json: renamed field future_timestamp_drift_seconds_post_v13 -> future_timestamp_drift_cap_seconds. ntp_required const flipped true -> false (NTP is NOT consensus-mandatory). Added ntp_recommended const true (locks the operational recommendation). Required[] array updated. (B) config/v13_release_candidate.json and website/api/v13_release_candidate.json: ntp_required false, ntp_recommended true, future_timestamp_drift_cap_seconds 30. (C) scripts/trinity/v13_release_candidate_check.py: REQUIRED_TOP_LEVEL extended; default report ntp_required False + ntp_recommended True; markdown report renders both flags as "NTP required (consensus)" and "NTP recommended (operational)". (D) Tests: const-lock test asserts ntp_required==False AND ntp_recommended==True; test_bad_ntp_required_rejected now asserts True is rejected; new test_bad_ntp_recommended_rejected asserts False is rejected. Full trinity: 1861/1861 PASS, 38 skipped (+1 new test). (E) website/sost-explorer.html banner: pill -> "UPDATE V13 BINARY + NTP RECOMMENDED BY 12,000"; main paragraph disambiguated: binary swap is consensus-mandatory, NTP is strongly recommended (not consensus-mandatory) with the 30 s drift cap explained. (F) config/v13_activation.json operator_warning text: NTP strongly recommended, clock skew > 30 s rejection (not consensus-mandatory). ZERO consensus rule change. Field rename + flag clarification only.
